question: Using FY2025 Connectivity segment figures, calculate YoY growth in revenue, operating income, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA. What do the differential growth rates imply about operating leverage within Connectivity?

core_facts.fact: Connectivity segment revenue was $11,387M in FY2025
Connectivity segment revenue was $7,599M in FY2024
Connectivity segment revenue grew 49.85% YoY
Connectivity segment operating income was $4,423M in FY2025
Connectivity segment operating income was $2,006M in FY2024
Connectivity segment operating income grew 120.49% YoY
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $7,168M in FY2025
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA was $3,849M in FY2024
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA grew 86.23% YoY
Connectivity segment operating margin was 38.84% in FY2025
Connectivity segment operating margin was 26.40% in FY2024
Connectivity segment operating margin expanded by 1,244 basis points YoY.
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA margin was 62.95% in FY2025
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA margin was 50.65% in FY2024
Connectivity Segment Adjusted EBITDA margin expanded by 1,230 bps YoY
The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under common-control accounting.
The segment figures reflect the recast consolidated entity, not standalone SpaceX.
Connectivity segment impairment charge was $39M in FY 2024.
Connectivity segment impairment was $0M in FY 2025.

question: Estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts. State all assumptions and identify what the filing’s subscriber and revenue definitions exclude from this ARPU estimate.

core_facts.fact: Q1 2026 Connectivity Segment Revenue was $3,257 million.
Consumer Revenue was $2,148 million in Q1 2026.
Connectivity revenue from Enterprise & Government customers was $1,109 million in Q1 2026.
Products Revenue from Starlink Kit Sales was $380 million in Q1 2026.
Starlink subscribers were 10.3 million as of March 31, 2026.
Starlink had 8.9 million subscribers as of December 31, 2025.
Managed enterprise customers with negotiated contracts (aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed-site enterprise) are excluded from the Starlink Subscriber count.
Multiple people sharing one Service Line are counted as one Starlink subscriber.
Starlink Subscriber ARPU was $66 per month for Q1 2026.
Starlink defines a 'Subscriber' as a unique Service Line, not an individual end user.
Hardware sales are excluded from the Starlink Subscriber ARPU numerator.

question: Calculate AI and Connectivity segment capex intensity for FY2025 and Q1 2026. Compare quarter versus full-year intensity and assess what the change implies about the pace and stage of AI infrastructure buildout.

core_facts.fact: The SpaceX S-1 Registration Statement was filed on May 20, 2026.
All figures were retrospectively recast under common-control accounting to include xAI and X Holdings for all periods.
AI Segment FY2025 revenue was $3,201M
AI Segment Q1 2026 revenue was $818M.
AI Segment revenue was $727M in Q1 2025
Connectivity segment FY2025 revenue was $11,387M
Connectivity segment Q1 2026 revenue was $3,257M.
AI Segment capital expenditures were $12,727M in FY2025.
AI Segment capital expenditures were $7,723M in Q1 2026.
AI Segment Q1 2025 Capex was $2,567M
Connectivity Segment FY2025 capital expenditures were $4,178M.
Connectivity Segment capital expenditures were $1,332M in Q1 2026.
The company expects a multi-year investment horizon before achieving sustained positive AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA.
